name: High Class Financial Solutions
description_short: Pre-settlement legal funding provider offering non-recourse cash advances to personal injury plaintiffs awaiting lawsuit settlements, with no credit check or monthly payments required.
description_long:
High Rise Financial (operating as High Class Financial Solutions) provides pre-settlement legal funding to individuals involved in pending personal injury lawsuits who need immediate cash while their cases are being litigated. The company positions itself as an alternative to traditional lawsuit loans, emphasizing that funding is contingent on case settlement rather than the borrower's creditworthiness or employment status. They serve clients who have been injured in accidents and are waiting for settlement proceeds, targeting those facing financial hardship during the litigation process.

The company offers advance funding ranging from $500 to $100,000 based on case merit rather than credit score. Their core service involves contacting the applicant's attorney, evaluating case strength, and if approved, providing cash within 24 hours. The funding is non-recourse—if the lawsuit is lost, the applicant owes nothing. Repayment with an agreed-upon interest rate occurs only when the settlement is finalized. They advertise no credit checks, no financial review, no job requirement, and no monthly payments during the waiting period.

What distinguishes High Rise is their explicit non-recourse structure and rapid funding timeline (24-48 hours to approval, same-day cash delivery possible). They emphasize transparency and claim to handle all paperwork coordination with attorneys. Customer testimonials highlight fast funding, friendly service, and one-day cash deposits. The company operates with bilingual support (Spanish language services available) and targets law firms as referral partners through a dedicated landing page.

However, this product carries important caveats: it is a contingent loan with interest fees that reduce net settlement proceeds, appeals to borrowers in financially vulnerable positions, and requires attorney coordination. While non-recourse structure eliminates default risk, the cost of capital is embedded in settlement recovery, meaning clients receive less from their final settlement. The absence of credit requirements reflects underwriting based on case strength rather than creditworthiness, making this suitable primarily for pending lawsuit plaintiffs with solid cases, not general personal finance borrowers.
pros: Non-recourse funding—no repayment obligation if lawsuit is lost
Fast approval and funding: 24-48 hours to approval, same-day cash delivery claimed
No credit check, financial review, employment verification, or job requirement
No monthly payments during litigation period; repayment contingent on settlement
Funding range up to $100,000 based on case merit rather than credit score
Attorney coordination handled by company; applicants need only sign agreement
cons: Interest/discount fees reduce net settlement proceeds received by plaintiff
Available only to personal injury lawsuit plaintiffs with pending cases—not general-purpose financing
Requires attorney cooperation and case evaluation; applicants cannot apply independently
Limited funding amount based on case settlement projection, not borrower need or income
Targets financially vulnerable populations (injured, out of work, debt-burdened during litigation)
best_for: Personal injury accident victims with pending lawsuits facing immediate financial hardship
Individuals out of work due to injury waiting for settlement with bills piling up
Plaintiffs with strong cases who need liquidity during multi-month litigation periods
Those unable to qualify for traditional loans due to lost income or damaged credit from accident recovery
diagnosis: High Rise Financial is best suited for personal injury plaintiffs with pending lawsuits who face immediate financial need and have strong cases likely to settle. The primary caveat is that this is not general-purpose consumer lending—it is contingent financing available only to lawsuit plaintiffs, and while non-recourse, it reduces net settlement recovery through interest/discount fees. This product solves cash flow problems during litigation but at the cost of settlement proceeds.
services: Non-recourse contingent funding (no repayment if lawsuit lost)
Same-day or next-day cash delivery to bank account
Interest rate negotiation and fee transparency
Law firm partnership and lien-based referral program
